content_en:
In my research, I have combined both theory, derived from reference books (of Ahadith) and practical experience; while relying on texts of the Qur’an and the Sunna to justify everything I have said.

I have divided this study into to two parts:

Book One is exclusively about Jinn and Demons, and is entitled: “Protection of Mankind from Jinn and Demons”.

Book Two is about sorcery and the way to thwart it, and so I have called it: “The Sharpest Weapon to Counteract Evil Sorcerers”.

Book One comprises six Chapters:

Chapter One: Jinn: a reality, not an illusion.

Chapter Two: Exorcism; a cure for sorcery.

Chapter Three: Satan’s attacks on the Prophets.

Chapter Four: Satan’s relations with mankind.

Chapter Five: Satan’s infiltration in corrupting hearts.

Chapter Six: Man’s protection against Satan.

I would like to urge you to accept all that conforms to the Qur’an and Sunnah in my research, but reject anything you may find that

contradicts them; for my heart is entirely open to any constructive criticism, based upon the Qur’an and Sunriah.

I would also like, in this introduction, to thank everyone who has helped me produce this book, especially our honourable Sheikh, Abd al-Khaleq Al-‘Attar, from whom I have learnt a great deal.

I ask Allah 3% to bless us with dediance to Him, in our words, deeds, and all our doings. “O Allah, we seek refuge in You from committing any act of polytheism in anything we have knowledge of, and we seek Your Forgiveness for anything we ignore. May Your Blessings and Peace be upon Your servant and Prophet, Muhammad, and his family and Companions.”

Wahid Abd al-Salam Bali

Chapter One: Jinn: a Reality, not an Illusion

Belief in the Unseen

One of the main roots of the Islamic faith is the belief in the Unseen; it is the first attribute that Allah 3 has given His Righteous Servants in His Book. He 4 says: (Alif Lam Mim. That is the Book, without any doubt. It contains guidance for those who fear Allah (the pious and righteous people): those who believe in the Unseen and establish salah and spend of what We have provided for them)’.

Therefore, it is obligatory upon every Muslim to believe in the Unseen, without any shadow of doubt. The Unseen is that which is not in our sight; yet Allah 3% and His Prophet 38 have informed us about it, as was narrated by Ibn Mas’ud

Jinn is part of the Unseen and we must believe in their existence, following the evidence confirming it in the Qur’an and the Sunnah.

1. (We diverted a group of Jinn towards you to listen to the Qur’an)’.

2. €O company of Jinn and men, did not Messengers come to you from among yourselves relating My Signs to you and warning you of the encounter of this Day of yours?)’.

‘ Surat al-Baqarah, verse 1. ? Tafsir Yon Kathir (1/41).

3 Surat al-Ahqaf, verse 29. ‘Surat al-An‘am, verse 130.

3. €O company of Jinn and men, if you are able to pierce through the confines of the heavens and earth, pierce through. You will not pierce through except with a clear authority)’.

4. (Say: ‘It has been revealed to me that a (small) group of Jinn listened and said, ‘We have heard a most amazing Recitation)”.

(1). Muslim reported that Ibn Mas‘ud # said: “We were in the company of the Messenger of Allah 4 one night and missed him. We searched for him in the valleys and hills and said (to ourselves): ‘He has either been taken away (by Jinn) or has been secretly killed.’ He (the narrator) said: “We spent the worst night that people could ever have spent. At dawn, we saw him coming from the side of Hira’.” He (the narrator) reported: “We said: “Messenger of Allah 3, we missed you and searched for you, but we could not find you, so we spent the worst night that people could ever have spent.” He 4 said: “There came to me a petitioner on behalf of the Jinn, and so I went with him and recited the Qur’an to them (Jinn).” He (the narrator) said: “He then went with us and showed us their tracks and the traces of their embers. They (the Jinn) asked him (the Prophet) about their provision and he said: “Every bone on which the name of Allah is recited is your provision. By the time it falls into your hands it shall be covered with flesh, and the dung of (the camels) is fodder for your animals.” The Messenger of Allah #8 said: “Don’t perform istinja with these (things)

for these are the food of your brothers (the Jinn)”.

(2). Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri narrated that the Prophet 3 once told him: “I see you like sheep and the wilderness. So whenever you are with your

' Surat al-Rahman, verse 33. ? Surat al-Jinn, vesre 1. > Surat al-Jinn, verse 6. “Recorded by Muslim.

sheep or in the wilderness and you want to make Adhan for prayers, raise your voice in doing so; for whoever hears the Adhan, whether a human being, Jinn or any other creature, will be a witness for you on the Day of Resurrection.”!

(3). Ibn ‘Abbas narrated that the Prophet once set out with the intention of going to Sug ‘Ukaz (the market of ‘Ukaz) along with some of his Companions. At the same time, a barrier was established between the devils and news from heaven. Fire began to be thrown at them. The devils went to their people, who asked them: ‘What is wrong with you?’ They replied: ‘A barrier has been placed between us and news from heaven, and fire has been thrown at us.’ They said: “Whatever has put a barrier between you and news from heaven must be something which has happened recently. Go eastward and westward and see what has set a barrier between you and news from heaven.” Those who went towards Tihama came across the Prophet at a place called Nakhla, which was on the way to Suq ‘Ukaz. The Prophet # was offering the Fajr prayer with his companions. When they heard the Qur’an they listened to it and said: ‘By Allah, this is the thing which has put a barrier between us and news from heaven.’ They went to their people and said: ‘O our people, verily we have heard a wonderful recital (Qur’an) which shows the true path; we believe in it and will not ascribe partners to our Lord.’ Allah then revealed the following verses to His Prophet (Surah Al-Jinn) (72): (Say: It has been revealed to me), and what was revealed to him was the conversation of the Jinn.?

There are many other proofs which you will find later on in this study,

Inability to see the Jinn is not a proof (of their nonexistence)

There are many other things that we cannot see, yet still exist. We cannot see an electrical current flowing through a wire, yet we confirm its existence as we see its effect in lights and other electrical things. The air that we breath and live on is unseen, yet we can feel it. The spirit forms the basis of our life, by which we live, and without which

' Recorded by al-Bukhari, Malik, al-Nasa’i, and Ibn Majah. ? Recorded in the two Sahih books.

we would die, yet we still do not know its essence, but believe in its existence.

From what material were the Jinn created?

Verses from the Qur’an and Ahadith from the Sunnah give clear-cut proof that the Jinn were created from fire.

Allah 3 said: (And He created the Jinn from a fusion of fire)'. Ibn ‘Abbas % said, explaining (from a fusion of fire): “It is from pure fire”, and in another narration, he said: “from its flame.”?

Allah 3¢ said: (We created the jinn before from the fire of a searing wind)’.

Iblis said: {I am better than him; You created me from fire but You created him from clay)‘. If it be said: “How can you make the saying of Iblis a proof, knowing that he lies?” We say: “The proof is not in the saying itself but in its confirmation by Allah 4%, and Allah 3 does not confirm falsehood.”

‘Aishah # reported that the Prophet 3 said: “The Angels were born from light, the Jinn were born from the spark of fire, and Adam was born as he has been described (in the Qur’an) for you (i.e. he was fashioned from clay).”*

If the Jinn were created from fire, then how could the unbelievers among them be punished with fire?

This question has occured to many people, but if they should reflect upon it they would find the answer and understand the situation better:

We all know that human beings were created from clay, but in their present form they are not of clay, so it is only their origin which is

1 Surat al-Rahman, verse 15.

? Tafsir Ibn Kathir (4/271).

3 Surat al-Hijr, verse 27.

4 Surat al-A ‘raf, verse 12.

5 Recorded by Muslim (18/123 — Tafsir al-Nawawi).

from clay. Likewise, Jinn were created from fire, but they are not in the form of fire now. There are many proofs to support this statement.

- ‘Aishah & reported that once the Prophet 4 was performing salah when a demon tried to attack him. The Prophet # overpowered him and strangled him, and said: ‘I strangled him until I sensed the coldness of his tongue in my hands’!. Now it is clear from this Hadith that Jinn, in their actual form, are not fire, otherwise the Prophet 3 would not have sensed any coldness of the tongue of one of them.

- Yahya related to me from Malik that Yahya ibn Sa‘id said: “When the Messenger of Allah 4 was taken on the Night Journey (Isra’), he saw a demon from the Jinn seeking him with a torch of fire. Whenever the Messenger of Allah 3 turned, he saw him. Jibril #8 asked him: ‘Shall I teach you some words to say? When you say them, his torch will be put out and will fall from him.’ The Messenger of Allah #% replied: “Yes, indeed.’ Jibril #8 said: ‘Say, ‘I seek refuge with the Noble Face of Allah and. with the complete words of Allah, which neither the good person nor the corrupt can exceed, from the evil of what descends from the sky arid the evil of what ascends through it, and from the evil of what is created on the earth and the evil of what comes out of it, and from the trials of the night and day, and from the visitations of the night and day, except for one that appears with good, O Merciful One!’”?
content_id:
Dalam penelitian ini, saya telah menggabungkan antara teori, yang bersumber dari kitab-kitab referensi (Ahadith) dan pengalaman praktis; dengan bersandar pada teks-teks Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah untuk membenarkan segala yang saya sampaikan.

Saya telah membagi kajian ini menjadi dua bagian:

Buku Pertama secara khusus membahas jin dan setan, berjudul: "Perlindungan Manusia dari Jin dan Setan".

Buku Kedua membahas sihir dan cara menangkalnya, sehingga saya menamakannya: "Senjata Paling Ampuh untuk Menangkal Penyihir Jahat".

Buku Pertama terdiri dari enam Bab:

Bab Pertama: Jin: suatu kenyataan, bukan ilusi.

Bab Kedua: Ruqyah; obat untuk sihir.

Bab Ketiga: Serangan Setan terhadap para Nabi.

Bab Keempat: Hubungan Setan dengan manusia.

Bab Kelima: Infiltrasi Setan dalam merusak hati.

Bab Keenam: Perlindungan manusia dari Setan.

Saya ingin mengajak Anda untuk menerima segala yang sesuai dengan Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah dalam penelitian saya, namun menolak apa pun yang mungkin Anda temukan yang

bertentangan dengan keduanya; karena hati saya sepenuhnya terbuka untuk setiap kritik membangun yang berlandaskan Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah.

Saya juga ingin, dalam pengantar ini, mengucapkan terima kasih kepada semua pihak yang telah membantu saya dalam menyusun buku ini, khususnya kepada Syaikh kami yang terhormat, Abd al-Khaleq Al-'Attar, yang darinya saya banyak menimba ilmu.

Saya memohon kepada Allah agar melimpahi kita ketaatan kepada-Nya, dalam ucapan, perbuatan, dan seluruh amal kita. "Ya Allah, kami berlindung kepada-Mu dari berbuat syirik dalam hal apa pun yang kami ketahui, dan kami memohon ampunan-Mu atas apa yang kami tidak ketahui. Semoga shalawat dan salam senantiasa tercurah kepada hamba dan Nabi-Mu, Muhammad, beserta keluarga dan para Sahabatnya."

Wahid Abd al-Salam Bali

Bab Pertama: Jin: Suatu Kenyataan, Bukan Ilusi

Iman kepada Yang Gaib

Salah satu pokok utama iman dalam Islam adalah iman kepada yang gaib; itulah sifat pertama yang Allah berikan kepada hamba-hamba-Nya yang saleh dalam Kitab-Nya. Dia berfirman: (Alif Lam Mim. Itulah Kitab yang tidak ada keraguan di dalamnya. Ia menjadi petunjuk bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa: yaitu mereka yang beriman kepada yang gaib, mendirikan salah, dan menginfakkan sebagian dari apa yang Kami rezekikan kepada mereka)'.

Maka wajib bagi setiap Muslim untuk beriman kepada yang gaib, tanpa keraguan sedikit pun. Yang gaib adalah sesuatu yang tidak tampak oleh pandangan kita; namun Allah dan Nabi-Nya telah memberitahukan kepada kita tentangnya, sebagaimana diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Mas'ud.

Jin termasuk bagian dari yang gaib dan kita wajib meyakini keberadaannya, mengikuti dalil-dalil yang membenarkannya dalam Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah.

1. (Kami arahkan sekelompok jin kepadamu untuk mendengarkan Al-Qur'an)'.

2. (Wahai golongan jin dan manusia, bukankah telah datang kepada kalian rasul-rasul dari kalangan kalian sendiri yang menyampaikan ayat-ayat-Ku dan memperingatkan kalian tentang pertemuan hari ini?)'.

' Surat al-Baqarah, ayat 1. ? Tafsir Ibn Katsir (1/41).

3 Surat al-Ahqaf, ayat 29. 'Surat al-An'am, ayat 130.

3. (Wahai golongan jin dan manusia, jika kalian sanggup menembus penjuru langit dan bumi, maka tembuslah. Kalian tidak akan mampu menembusnya kecuali dengan kekuatan yang nyata)'.

4. (Katakanlah: 'Telah diwahyukan kepadaku bahwa sekelompok kecil jin mendengarkan lalu berkata, 'Sesungguhnya kami telah mendengar bacaan yang sangat menakjubkan')".

(1). Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn Mas'ud berkata: "Kami pernah bersama Rasulullah pada suatu malam, lalu beliau tidak tampak oleh kami. Kami mencarinya di lembah-lembah dan bukit-bukit dan berkata (dalam hati): 'Beliau telah dibawa pergi (oleh jin) atau telah dibunuh secara diam-diam.' Ia (perawi) berkata: 'Kami menghabiskan malam paling buruk yang pernah dirasakan oleh manusia.' Saat fajar tiba, kami melihat beliau datang dari arah Hira'." Ia (perawi) berkata: "Kami berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, kami kehilangan Anda dan telah mencari, namun tidak menemukan, sehingga kami menghabiskan malam paling buruk yang pernah dirasakan manusia.' Beliau bersabda: 'Seorang utusan dari jin mendatangiku, maka aku pergi bersamanya dan membacakan Al-Qur'an kepada mereka (jin).'" Ia (perawi) berkata: "Kemudian beliau pergi bersama kami dan memperlihatkan jejak mereka dan sisa-sisa bara api mereka. Mereka (para jin) bertanya kepada beliau tentang bekal mereka, dan beliau bersabda: 'Setiap tulang yang disebutkan nama Allah atasnya adalah bekal kalian. Ketika jatuh ke tangan kalian, tulang itu akan tertutup daging, dan kotoran (unta) adalah pakan untuk hewan-hewan kalian.' Rasulullah bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian beristinja dengan benda-benda ini,

karena itu adalah makanan saudara-saudara kalian (para jin)'."

(2). Abu Sa'id al-Khudri meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi pernah berkata kepadanya: "Aku melihat engkau menyukai domba dan padang terbuka. Maka kapan pun engkau bersama

' Surat al-Rahman, ayat 33. ? Surat al-Jinn, ayat 1. > Surat al-Jinn, ayat 6. "Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.

domba-dombamu atau di padang terbuka dan engkau hendak mengumandangkan adzan untuk shalat, keraskan suaramu dalam melakukannya; karena siapa pun yang mendengar adzan, baik manusia, jin, maupun makhluk lainnya, akan menjadi saksi bagimu pada Hari Kebangkitan."!

(3). Ibn 'Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi pernah berangkat dengan niat menuju Suq 'Ukaz (pasar 'Ukaz) bersama sebagian Sahabatnya. Pada saat yang sama, sebuah penghalang dipasang antara para setan dan berita dari langit. Api pun mulai dilemparkan kepada mereka. Para setan kembali kepada kaumnya, yang bertanya: 'Ada apa dengan kalian?' Mereka menjawab: 'Telah dipasang penghalang antara kami dan berita dari langit, dan api dilemparkan kepada kami.' Mereka berkata: 'Apa pun yang memasang penghalang antara kalian dan berita dari langit pasti sesuatu yang baru terjadi. Pergilah ke timur dan barat dan lihatlah apa yang telah memasang penghalang antara kalian dan berita dari langit.' Mereka yang pergi ke arah Tihama bertemu dengan Nabi di suatu tempat bernama Nakhla, yang terletak di jalan menuju Suq 'Ukaz. Nabi sedang mengerjakan shalat Fajar bersama para Sahabatnya. Ketika mereka mendengar Al-Qur'an, mereka mendengarkannya dan berkata: 'Demi Allah, inilah yang telah memasang penghalang antara kami dan berita dari langit.' Mereka pergi kepada kaumnya dan berkata: 'Wahai kaum kami, sesungguhnya kami telah mendengar bacaan yang menakjubkan (Al-Qur'an) yang menunjukkan jalan yang benar; kami beriman kepadanya dan tidak akan mempersekutukan sesuatu pun dengan Tuhan kami.' Allah kemudian menurunkan ayat-ayat berikut kepada Nabi-Nya (Surah Al-Jinn) (72): (Katakanlah: Telah diwahyukan kepadaku), dan yang diwahyukan kepadanya adalah percakapan para jin.?

Masih banyak lagi bukti-bukti lain yang akan Anda temukan dalam kajian ini.

Ketidakmampuan melihat jin bukanlah bukti (atas ketiadaan mereka)

Banyak hal lain yang tidak dapat kita lihat, namun tetap ada. Kita tidak dapat melihat arus listrik yang mengalir melalui kabel, namun kita memastikan keberadaannya ketika kita melihat pengaruhnya pada lampu dan perangkat listrik lainnya. Udara yang kita hirup dan kita gunakan untuk hidup tidak tampak, namun kita dapat merasakannya. Ruh merupakan pondasi kehidupan kita, yang dengannya kita hidup, dan tanpanya

' Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari, Malik, al-Nasa'i, dan Ibn Majah. ? Diriwayatkan dalam dua kitab Shahih.

kita akan mati, namun kita tetap tidak mengetahui hakikatnya, meski meyakini keberadaannya.

Dari bahan apakah jin diciptakan?

Ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an dan Ahadith dari Sunnah memberikan bukti yang jelas bahwa jin diciptakan dari api.

Allah berfirman: (Dan Dia menciptakan jin dari nyala api)'. Ibn 'Abbas berkata, menjelaskan (dari nyala api): "Yaitu dari api yang murni", dan dalam riwayat lain beliau berkata: "dari lidah apinya."?

Allah berfirman: (Dan jin telah Kami ciptakan sebelumnya dari api yang sangat panas)'.

Iblis berkata: {Aku lebih baik darinya; Engkau menciptakanku dari api sedangkan Engkau menciptakannya dari tanah)'. Jika dikatakan: "Bagaimana engkau menjadikan ucapan Iblis sebagai dalil, padahal ia berdusta?" Kami menjawab: "Dalilnya bukan pada ucapan itu sendiri, melainkan pada penguatan Allah atasnya, dan Allah tidak membenarkan kebatilan."

'Aisyah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda: "Para Malaikat diciptakan dari cahaya, jin diciptakan dari percikan api, dan Adam diciptakan sebagaimana yang telah digambarkan (dalam Al-Qur'an) kepada kalian (yakni ia dibentuk dari tanah liat)."*

Jika jin diciptakan dari api, lalu bagaimana orang-orang kafir di antara mereka dapat disiksa dengan api?

Pertanyaan ini telah muncul di benak banyak orang, namun jika mereka merenungkannya niscaya akan menemukan jawabannya dan lebih memahami persoalannya:

Kita semua tahu bahwa manusia diciptakan dari tanah liat, namun dalam wujudnya saat ini mereka bukanlah tanah liat, sehingga hanya asal-usul mereka saja yang

1 Surat al-Rahman, ayat 15.

? Tafsir Ibn Kathir (4/271).

3 Surat al-Hijr, ayat 27.

4 Surat al-A'raf, ayat 12.

5 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (18/123 — Tafsir al-Nawawi).

dari tanah liat. Demikian pula, Jin diciptakan dari api, namun sekarang mereka tidak dalam wujud api. Terdapat banyak dalil yang mendukung pernyataan ini.

- 'Aishah & meriwayatkan bahwa suatu ketika Nabi # sedang melaksanakan salah ketika seekor setan mencoba menyerangnya. Nabi # mengalahkannya dan mencekiknya, lalu bersabda: 'Aku mencekiknya hingga aku merasakan dinginnya lidahnya di tanganku'! Kini jelaslah dari hadith ini bahwa Jin, dalam wujud aslinya, bukanlah api, sebab jika demikian Nabi # tidak akan merasakan dinginnya lidah salah satu dari mereka.

- Yahya meriwayatkan kepadaku dari Malik bahwa Yahya ibn Sa'id berkata: "Ketika Rasulullah # diperjalankan pada Malam Isra', beliau melihat setan dari golongan Jin yang mengejarnya dengan obor api. Ke mana pun Rasulullah # berpaling, beliau melihatnya. Jibril #8 bertanya kepadanya: 'Maukah aku ajarkan kepadamu beberapa kalimat untuk diucapkan? Apabila kamu mengucapkannya, obornya akan padam dan jatuh darinya.' Rasulullah #% menjawab: 'Ya, tentu saja.' Jibril #8 berkata: 'Ucapkanlah, "Aku berlindung dengan Wajah Allah Yang Mulia dan dengan kalimat-kalimat Allah yang sempurna, yang tidak dapat dilampaui oleh orang baik maupun orang jahat, dari kejahatan apa yang turun dari langit dan kejahatan apa yang naik melaluinya, dan dari kejahatan apa yang diciptakan di bumi dan kejahatan apa yang keluar darinya, dan dari fitnah malam dan siang, dan dari gangguan malam dan siang, kecuali yang datang membawa kebaikan, wahai Dzat Yang Maha Pengasih!'"?
content_ar: In my research, I have combined both theory, derived from reference الكتب (of الأحاديث) and practical experience; بينما relying on texts of the Qur’an and the Sunna to justify everything I have said. I have divided this study into to two parts: الكتاب One is exclusively about الجن and Demons, and is entitled: “Protection of Mankind from الجن and Demons”. الكتاب Two is about sorcery and the الطريق to thwart it, and لذا I have called it: “The Sharpest Weapon to Counteract الشر Sorcerers”. الكتاب One comprises six الأبواب: الباب One: الجن: a reality, not an illusion. الباب Two: Exorcism; a الدواء for sorcery. Chapter Three: الشيطان’s attacks on the الأنبياء. الباب Four: الشيطان’s relations with mankind. الباب Five: الشيطان’s infiltration in corrupting القلوب. الباب Six: Man’s protection against الشيطان. I would like to urge you to accept all that conforms to the Qur’an and السنة in my research, but reject anything you may find that contradicts them; for my القلب is entirely open to any constructive criticism, based upon the Qur’an and Sunriah. I would أيضاً like, in this introduction, to thank everyone who has helped me produce this الكتاب, ولا سيما our honourable Sheikh, Abd al-Khaleq Al-‘Attar, from whom I have learnt a great deal. I ask الله 3% to bless us with dediance to Him, in our words, deeds, and all our doings. “O الله, we seek refuge in You from committing any act of polytheism in anything we have العلم of, and we seek Your المغفرة for anything we ignore. May Your البركات and Peace be upon Your servant and النبي, محمد ﷺ, and his الأسرة and الصحابة.” Wahid Abd al-Salam Bali الباب One: الجن: a Reality, not an Illusion الاعتقاد in the Unseen One of the الرئيسي roots of the Islamic الإيمان is the الاعتقاد in the Unseen; it is the أولاً attribute that الله 3 has given His الصالح Servants in His الكتاب. He 4 says: (Alif Lam Mim. That is the الكتاب, without any doubt. It contains الهداية for those who الخوف الله (the pious and الصالح people): those who believe in the Unseen and establish الصلاة and spend of what We have provided for them)’. لذلك, يجب upon every مسلم to believe in the Unseen, without any shadow of doubt. The Unseen is that which is not in our sight; yet الله 3% and His النبي 38 have informed us about it, as was narrated by Ibn Mas’ud الجن is part of the Unseen and علينا believe in their existence, following the evidence confirming it in the Qur’an and the السنة. 1. (We diverted a group of الجن towards you to listen to the Qur’an)’. 2. €O company of الجن and men, did not الرسل come to you from among yourselves relating My Signs to you and warning you of the encounter of this Day of yours?)’. ‘ Surat al-Baqarah, الآية 1. ? التفسير Yon Kathir (1/41). 3 Surat al-Ahqaf, الآية 29. ‘Surat al-An‘am, الآية 130. 3. €O company of J
